relationships.description: A least-privilege access requirement is typically satisfied by implementing role-based access control that limits each user to only the access their role needs.
A requirement to encrypt data at rest is satisfied by a control that enforces disk- or database-level encryption for stored data.
A segregation-of-duties requirement is satisfied by a control requiring two different individuals to initiate and approve a sensitive transaction.
A role-based access control's operation is evidenced by a periodic access review report showing granted access matches assigned roles.
An encryption control's operation is evidenced by a configuration scan confirming encryption is enabled on the relevant systems.
A dual-approval control's operation is evidenced by an audit log showing two distinct approvers for each transaction sampled.

relationships.subject_label: Unauthorized access to systems or data
relationships.description: The risk of unauthorized access is mitigated by role-based access control limiting who can reach a system or dataset.
The risk of a third-party data breach is mitigated by assessing a vendor's security posture before and during the relationship.
The risk of a fraudulent transaction is mitigated by requiring dual approval before a transaction is executed.

relationships.object_label: Encrypt sensitive data in transit and at rest
relationships.description: The NCA Essential Cybersecurity Controls impose an obligation to encrypt sensitive data both in transit and at rest.

relationships.object_label: Obtain data subject consent before processing
relationships.description: The PDPL imposes an obligation to obtain a data subject's consent (or another lawful basis) before processing their personal data.
An organization's Access Control Policy is the document through which the least-privilege access requirement is formally established.
An organization's Data Retention Policy is the document through which a defined retention period requirement is formally established.
